title: Strict module privacy + bare-prefix import (full R7RS library hygiene)
goal: Close the last two gaps in R7RS library support: (1) strict module privacy — bindings not in `(provide …)` are actually inaccessible from importers (currently `(provide …)` is informational); (2) `(import (prefix (m) p-))` over a module's whole export list (currently requires an explicit `only`/`rename`). Both gaps share the same architectural fix: defer alias creation past parser-time to require-resolution time when the module's full export list is known.
next_actions: Extend the require-resolution pipeline (exe/eshkol-run.cpp::process_requires) to enumerate each module's exports at load time. Currently process_requires loads the file; add a step that walks its top-level (provide …) form and exposes the export list to downstream alias creation.

Remove the `R7RS prefix import currently requires an explicit only or rename set` guard at lib/frontend/parser.cpp:3242. Replace with a deferred 'prefix-all-exports' directive that the require-resolver applies after loading.

Strict privacy: bindings not in (provide …) get a unique internal name (linkage-mangled or renamed in the imported module's local symbol table) so importers can't reach them by their bare original name. Existing top-level identity within the defining module is preserved.
tests/v1_3_edge_cases/prefix_import_test.esk: (import (prefix (m1) m1-)) over a module with 3 exports — verify all three are accessible as m1-name.
tests/v1_3_edge_cases/module_privacy_test.esk: a module with (provide foo) but not bar; verify the importer can call foo but a reference to bar fails with 'unbound variable'.
Update docs/breakdown/SCHEME_COMPATIBILITY.md: section §5.2 import — flip from ⚠️ to ✅, drop the 'strict hiding still limited' caveat.
acceptance: (import (prefix (m1) m1-)) makes every export of m1 accessible as m1-name with no explicit only/rename required.
A binding defined in m1 but not in m1's (provide …) is unreachable from the importer (calling it raises unbound-variable).
Existing v1.2 (require core.list.transform) and friends still work unchanged.
ux_payoff: Closes the last marked v1.3 R7RS item. Eshkol becomes a strict R7RS library implementation.
